payload.name: 250601 Agentic AI what are those?
payload.text_content:
VIDEO3



VIDEO2


VIDEO





avtar video:  

ideo Script (Agentic AI Confusion) – under 150 words
🎤 Hook (Negative + Funny):
 “So you think Agentic AI is just a super-intelligent chatbot? Yeah… no. That’s like saying the delivery guy cooked your food.” 🍔🤖
👀 Main Content:
 Most people confuse agentic AI with AI models like ChatGPT or Claude. But here’s the truth—agents aren’t the models. They’re the managers that tell different models what to do and when to do it.
Think of it like this:
 Your food delivery app didn’t make your biryani—it just coordinated the restaurant, driver, and map. That’s what agents do—they connect models, tools, APIs, even browsers, and make them work together to get things done.
Agentic AI = smart project manager.
 Models = talented employees.
 You = still confused? Hit follow, and I’ll make AI sound human, not robotic.
🎬 CTA:
 Don’t fear the agents—they’re just here to organize the chaos, not take over.


Instagram Description
 Agentic AI isn’t just another chatbot. It’s the smart coordinator that tells models like ChatGPT, APIs, and tools what to do—like a project manager for AI.
 Most people confuse agents with the models themselves, but they’re not the same thing. Think of agents as delivery apps—they don’t make the food, they just make sure it gets done.
 If you’re a product manager, engineer, or student trying to make sense of AI buzzwords, this one’s for you.
 Follow for more clear, simple breakdowns of complex AI terms—no jargon, no drama.
LinkedIn Description
 Confused between agentic AI and models like ChatGPT? You’re not alone.
 Agentic AI is the system that coordinates—connecting multiple tools, models, and APIs to complete tasks across platforms. It’s not the chef; it’s the one who makes sure dinner gets served.
 If you’re working in product, engineering, or just exploring GenAI in depth, understanding agentic workflows is critical to building smarter applications.
 Follow for more AI breakdowns that speak your language—practical, simple, and relevant to your work.
